*** 1 FATAL *** THE MISSOURI HIGHWAY PATROL RELAYED TO THE NWS THAT A 65 YEAR OLD MAN WAS SWEPT OFF OF THE ROAD AND DROWN BY A FLOODED CREEK NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 4740 AND COUNTY ROAD 5130. THIS SECTION OF THE ROAD WAS NOT A LOW WATER CROSSING...AND THE STREAM IS AN UNNAMED SMALL HEADWATER TRIBUTARY TO SPRING CREEK.

*** 122 FATAL, 750 INJ *** A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT AN EF-5 TORNADO IMPACTED A LARGE PORTION OF THE CITY OF JOPLIN. WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED IN EXCESS OF 200 MPH. THE PATH WIDTH WAS ESTIMATED AT THREE QUARTERS OF A MILE WIDE.

BETWEEN MILE MARKER 10 AND 12. TREES STIPED OF BARK. OVER 20 SEMITRUCKS AND CARS FLIPPED OFF I-44. ALL INTERSTATE SIGNS GONE AND DAMAGE.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 5 MILES EAST NORTHEAST OF DIAMOND MISSOURI...REMAINED ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 17 MILES...AND LIFTED APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES NORTH OF MONETT. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 120 MILES PER HOUR. THE TORNADO DAMAGE PATH HAD A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF ONE HALF MILE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED...4 MOBILE HOMES SUFFERED CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE OR WERE DESTROYED...AND NUMEROUS POWER POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN.

UPDATED END POINT. A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WINDS UP TO 120 MPH IMPACTED CENTRAL STONE COUNTY INTO NORTHWESTERN TANEY COUNTY. THE TORNADO TRACKED APPROXIATELY 16 MILES FROM 3 MILES NORTHWEST OF GALENA TO 1 MILE SOUTH OF SADDLE BROOKE. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS. THE TORNADO DOWNED TREES...DESTROYED SEVERAL TURKEY BARNS....DAMAGED A HOUSE AND MUTIPLE OUTBUILDINGS.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WINDS UP TO 120 MPH IMPACTED CENTRAL STONE COUNTY. THE TORNADO TRACKED APPROXIATELY 7 MILES FROM 3 MILES NORTHWEST OF GALENA TO 5 MILES NORTHEAST OF GALENA. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS. THE TORNADO DOWNED TREES...DESTROYED SEVERAL TURKEY BARNS....DAMAGED A HOUSE AND OUTBUILDINGS.

NUMEROUS SMALL BUSINESSES AND HOUSES FLOODED IN THE CENTER OF TOWN. FLOODING BEGAN AT 4 PM ON 23 MAY AND CRESTED AT 11 PM ON 23 MAY. FLOOD WATER HAD RECEEDED BACK IN TO CREEK BANKS AS OF 0400 AM ON 24 MAY.

ALONG THE PATH NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED OR DAMAGED. ONE HOME SUFFERED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES OCCURED.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 80-90 MPH OCCURRED ON JUNE 18TH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AROUND 1135 PM ABOUT 4 MILES NORTHEAST OF EVERTON AND TRAVELLED 11 MILES LIFTING AROUND 1150 PM ABOUT 6 MILES EAST OF WALNUT GROVE. THE STORM HAD A PATH WIDTH OF 100-200 YDS AND SNAPPED OR UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OF 80-90 MPH OCCURRED ON JUNE 18TH. THE STORM TOUCHED DOWN AROUND 1140 PM ABOUT 2 MILES SOUTHEAST OF DADEVILLE AND TRAVELLED 6 MILES LIFTING AROUND 1150 PM ABOUT 1 MILE SOUTH OF EUDORA. THIS STORM HAD A PATH WIDTH OF ABOUT 100 YARDS AND SNAPPED OR UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES AND DESTROYED A SMALL OUTBUILDING.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE ACROSS PORTIONS OF WEBB CITY. DAMAGE WAS FOCUSED IN A PATH TO THE EAST NORTHEAST BENDING A SIGN AND MOVING CART CORRALS AT WALMART...PULLING THE EVE OF A ROOF BACK ON A NEARBY STRIP MALL AND BLOWING TWO HVAC UNITS OFF A ROOF AND TOPPLING ANOTHER UNIT. A FEW SMALL TO MEDIUM SIZED TREES WERE BLOWN OVER...WITH NUMEROUS LIMBS DOWNED FROM WEBB CITY TO CARTHAGE. WIND SPEED IS ESTIMATED AT 70 TO 80 MPH.
A FEW TREES AND ONE POWER POLE DOWN WITHIN THE CITY LIMITS RESULTING IN POWER OUTAGES. A FEW SHINGLES BLOWN OFF A ROOF OF A HOUSE AND A WINDOW SHUTTER KNOCKED OFF OF A WINDOW.
